title: 10 best herbal capsules for cystitis
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for cystitis are natural remedies designed to alleviate the symptoms of this painful and common urinary tract infection. These capsules combine the healing properties of various herbs to provide relief from discomfort, pain, and frequent urination. The best herbal capsules for cystitis include Juniperus communis, Equisetum arvense, Hypericum perforatum, Berberis vulgaris, Astragalus membranaceus, Vaccinium macrocarpon, Cinchona officinalis, Silybum marianum, Sambucus nigra, and Taraxacum officinale. If left untreated, cystitis can lead to recurring infections, kidney damage, and even long-term health issues, affecting daily life, work, and relationships.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry capsules helps with cystitis because they contain a natural ingredient called uva-ursi, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Uva-ursi works by reducing inflammation in the urinary tract, thereby alleviating symptoms such as burning sensation while urinating and frequent trips to the bathroom. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help eliminate bacteria that can cause cystitis, promoting a healthy balance of gut flora and preventing future infections. As a result, bearberry capsules provide natural relief from the discomfort and pain associated with cystitis.
remedy_properties: Urinary antiseptic properties: Bearberry capsules contain arbutin, a natural compound that has antimicrobial effects against bacteria and fungi, helping to combat infections and alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in bearberry capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with cystitis.

Antioxidant properties: Bearberry's antioxidant compounds can help protect the urinary tract from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of chronic conditions like cystitis.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid helps with cystitis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial infections in the urinary tract.
Arbutin: This phenolic glycoside has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help prevent bacterial growth and reduce discomfort associated with cystitis.
Gallic acid: This phenolic acid has potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat infections and alleviate symptoms associated with cystitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in arbutin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and fights bacterial infections.
Fruits: Contain arbutin, which is converted to hydroquinone in the body, helping to alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
Barks: Also contain arbutin, making them a useful part of the plant for treating urinary tract infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of arctostaphylos uva ursi berries in late summer or early fall when they are ripe and dry.
Dry the berries in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind 1-2 cups of the dried berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of water to create a gel-like substance.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them using a gelatin capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with cystitis because they contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that coats the urinary tract and soothes the lining of the bladder. This reduces inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis. The capsule's anti-inflammatory properties also help to ease burning sensations during urination and alleviate pain. Additionally, marshmallow has natural antimicrobial properties that may help combat bacterial infections that contribute to cystitis. By providing a protective barrier and reducing inflammation, marshmallow capsules can provide relief from the symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow root contains a thick, protective mucilage that soothes and coats the bladder and urinary tract lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity: The antioxidants in marshmallow root help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and tissue damage in the urinary tract, while its anti-inflammatory compounds minimize swelling and pain.

Wetting agent properties: Marshmallow mucilage acts as a natural lubricant, helping to increase urine volume and frequency, making it easier for the body to flush out bacteria, toxins, and other irritants that can contribute to cystitis.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages in Althaea officinalis help to soothe and protect the bladder and urinary tract lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis.

Gallic Acid (Phenolic compound): Gallic Acid has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of cystitis.

Flavonoids (Specifically, Quercetin): Flavonoids in Althaea officinalis, particularly Quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and prevent further damage to the urinary tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and heal irritated tissues in the urinary tract.
Leaves: Althaea officinalis leaves contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
Stems: The stems of Althaea officinalis are also a source of mucilage, which can help protect and calm the mucous membranes in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 to 3 cups of dried root of althaea officinalis from a trusted supplier or cultivated source.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the ground root powder with 1 part of vegetable cellulose or gelatin capsules in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand for 15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature in a cool dry place for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with cystitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The herb's flavonoids and steroidal compounds help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms such as burning sensation while urinating and frequent trips to the bathroom. Additionally, stinging nettle's ability to relax the bladder muscles can help to ease pain and discomfort associated with cystitis. By addressing these underlying causes, stinging nettle capsules provide natural relief from cystitis symptoms, promoting a smoother and more comfortable digestive process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, terpenes, and carotenoids that help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of cystitis.

Antimicrobial properties: The plant's bioactive compounds, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have antimicrobial effects that help combat bacterial infections causing cystitis, reducing the risk of complications and promoting healing.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: Stinging nettle's astringent and antiseptic properties help soothe and protect the urinary tract lining, reducing discomfort and pain associated with cystitis while preventing further irritation.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the inflammation and irritation associated with cystitis, promoting a faster recovery.

Alkaloids (Urticines): The alkaloids in Urtica dioica, particularly 5-hydroxytryptamine (5-HT), possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate the pain and discomfort caused by cystitis.

Flavonoids (Quercetin): Flavonoids, such as quercetin, have potent antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the urinary tract, thereby preventing further irritation and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Urtica dioica to make capsules for cystitis due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms.
Roots: The roots are also used to make capsules for cystitis, as they contain alkaloids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are another part used to make capsules for cystitis, as they possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of the fresh plant material on a dry day at the peak of its potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 50c for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 20g per minute.
Mix the powder with 300g of gelatin capsules and fill them with 500mg each using a capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with cystitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with this condition. The compounds present in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause urinary tract infections, thus providing relief from symptoms like burning sensations while urinating and frequent urges to go. Additionally, ginger's natural antiseptic properties help to cleanse the urinary tract, promoting a healthy environment for overall bladder health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which can help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of cystitis such as burning pain and frequency.

Antibacterial properties: Herbal ginger capsules have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections, including those caused by E. coli, a common culprit behind cystitis, reducing the risk of complications and promoting healing.

Digestive soothing agents: Ginger's carminative properties can help alleviate digestive discomforts like bloating, cramps, and nausea often associated with cystitis, allowing for more comfortable urination and reduced stress on the body.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols also possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in combating bacterial infections that can cause cystitis.
Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis, such as pain and urinary frequency.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale due to their high concentration of compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Roots: The roots are also widely used due to their rich content of bioactive compounds that help in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections associated with cystitis.

Barks: Although less commonly used, some formulations may utilize the barks of Zingiber officinale for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in managing cystitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Dry the zingiber officinale rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2-3 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried zingiber officinale rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a carrier agent such as cellulose or starch in a 1:1 ratio to facilitate capsule filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using an encapsulation machine or a small spoon.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto capsules helps with cystitis because they support urinary health by reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms such as burning sensations while urinating, frequent urination, and pelvic pain. The herb's active compounds, including fatty acids and plant sterols, work to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, allowing for smoother urine flow and relieving pressure on the bladder. Additionally, saw palmetto may help reduce the growth of bacteria that can contribute to cystitis infections, promoting overall urinary health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Saw palmetto's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to cystitis symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and discomfort in the bladder and urethra, alleviating symptoms of cystitis such as burning during urination and pelvic pain.

Urethral relaxant properties: Saw palmetto may help relax the muscles in the urinary tract, including the urethra, which can improve urine flow and reduce the frequency and urgency of urination, common symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_constituents: Ismile: A type of phytosterol found in Serenoa repens, ismile has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections in the urinary tract.

Furostanol saponins: These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of cystitis, such as pain and discomfort, by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract.

Bergenic acid: This triterpenoid has been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause urinary tract infections and alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help alleviate cystitis symptoms.
Leaves: They are used because they contain bioactive compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can contribute to the treatment of cystitis.
Rhyzomes: They are used as they are rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections associated with cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of serenoa repens roots and dry them in a controlled environment for 2 weeks at 50°c to prevent spoilage.
Grind 200g of dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes to achieve uniform consistency.
Mix the powder with 100g of gelatin capsules in a ratio of 2:1 to fill the capsules completely.
Fill 200 gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine and seal them immediately.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corn capsules helps with cystitis because they provide a natural remedy to soothe and alleviate symptoms of this common condition. The herbal formula in these capsules works by reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis, allowing the body to heal faster and more effectively. Additionally, the corn extract has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections often linked to cystitis, providing relief from painful urination and frequent trips to the bathroom. By promoting a healthy urinary tract environment, corn capsules can help prevent recurring episodes of cystitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal corn capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, providing relief from symptoms such as burning and frequent urination.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in herbal corn capsules help protect the bladder lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic cystitis and improving overall bladder health.

Urinary antiseptic properties: Herbal corn capsules contain antimicrobial compounds that help combat bacterial infections in the urinary tract, reducing the severity and duration of cystitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Silica: Helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with cystitis.
Phytoestrogens (specifically, coumarins and lignans): May help to balance hormonal fluctuations and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to cystitis symptoms.
Saponins: Have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which could help to reduce bacterial loads and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make capsules for cystitis due to their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties.
Leaves: Zea mays leaves are used in herbal medicine for cystitis treatment due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Corn roots are also used in some herbal remedies for cystitis due to their potential diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of zea mays leaves and flowers at their peak potency and store them in a cool dry place.
Dry the zea mays material in a low-temperature dehydrator at 50°c for 24 hours to preserve its properties.
Grind 50g of dried zea mays into a fine powder using a high-speed mill with a 1mm screen size.
Mix the zea mays powder with 10g of gelatin and 10g of glycerin in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 200mg of the zea mays-gelatin-glycerin mixture each using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with cystitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract. The curcumin present in turmeric capsules has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause cystitis, reducing the severity of symptoms such as burning sensation while urinating and frequent urination. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect the bladder lining from damage caused by chronic inflammation, promoting healing and reducing discomfort associated with cystitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compound curcumin reduces inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as burning and frequency associated with cystitis.
Antimicrobial property: Turmeric's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that often contribute to cystitis, reducing the severity of symptoms and preventing further complications.

Uroprotective property: Curcumin in turmeric capsules has been shown to protect the bladder lining from damage caused by inflammation and oxidative stress, which can reduce the risk of long-term bladder damage and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Helps alleviate cystitis symptoms by reducing inflammation, modulating the immune system, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties against bacteria that cause urinary tract infections.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Demonstrates potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to its ability to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation associated with cystitis.

Volitin: Although not as widely studied as curcumin, volitin has shown potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that could help alleviate cystitis symptoms and combat underlying infections.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for making capsules due to their high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps in reducing symptoms of cystitis.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also used, as they contain a lower concentration of curcumin compared to rhyzomes but still provide some therapeutic benefits for cystitis.
Leaves: Although less commonly used, leaves of Curcuma longa may be used in some formulations for cystitis due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30g of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes and wash them thoroughly with clean water.
Dry the harvested curcuma longa rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 50-60°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried curcuma longa rhizomes into a fine powder using a grinder.
Mix 3g of the ground curcuma longa powder with 10g of gelatin powder and 10g of silica powder.
Fill the powder mixture into empty gelatin capsules and seal them tightly for consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with cystitis because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm the urinary tract. The diuretic properties of dandelion also increase urine production, which can help flush out bacteria and other toxins that may be causing the infection. Additionally, dandelion's ability to stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract can provide relief from cystitis symptoms such as burning while urinating and frequent urination.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxadiol, help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of cystitis like burning sensation while urinating.

Antioxidant property: Dandelion's antioxidant properties, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by chronic inflammation in the bladder, reducing the risk of tissue damage and scarring.

Urinary diuretic property: Dandelion's ability to increase urine production and flow helps flush out bacteria and toxins from the urinary tract, reducing bacterial growth and infection that can contribute to cystitis.
remedy_constituents: Apolypodol: A sesquiterpene lactone in Dandelion, which may help alleviate cystitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response.
Taraxasterol: A triterpene sterol in Dandelion, which may help with cystitis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties and potentially protecting the bladder lining from damage.
Inulin: A fructan polysaccharide in Dandelion, which may help with cystitis by promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, thereby supporting the overall health of the urinary tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of taraxasterol and other bioactive compounds, which may help alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
Seeds: Seeds are used because they contain taraxasterol and other saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help treat cystitis.
Roots: Roots are used as they contain inulin and other bioactive compounds, which may help soothe and protect the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in quantities sufficient for the desired batch size.
Dry the taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 400 milligrams of the powdered taraxacum officinale with 400 milligrams of a capsule filler in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule sealing device.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with cystitis because they contain berberine, a powerful antibacterial compound that inhibits the growth of bacteria responsible for urinary tract infections. Berberine also has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and pain associated with cystitis. Additionally, goldenseal's antifungal and antimicrobial properties help to eliminate fungal and viral infections that can exacerbate cystitis symptoms. By targeting multiple pathogens and alleviating inflammation, goldenseal capsules provide natural relief from the discomfort and pain of cystitis.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to combat bacterial infections that cause cystitis and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract.

Hydrastis canadensis (Goldenseal): The active compound berberine is contained in Goldenseal's roots and rhizomes, which have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections, including cystitis, due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Berberin: Berberin has a powerful antioxidant effect, which helps to protect the bladder and urinary tract from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and scarring.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (specifically Berberine): Berberine in Hydrastis canadensis has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the bacterial load and inflammation associated with cystitis.

Iridoid Glycosides (specifically Aucubin and Aucubigenin): These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe and protect the bladder lining from further irritation and damage.

Phenolic Acids (specifically Gallic Acid and Ellagic Acid): Phenolic acids in Hydrastis canadensis have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain berberine, a compound with antimicrobial properties that help alleviate cystitis symptoms.
Leaves: They also contain berberine and other alkaloids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects in treating cystitis.
Barks: The bark of Hydrastis canadensis contains berberine and other compounds that help soothe and protect the urinary tract, making it effective in treating cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500mg of dried hydrastis canadensis root and 1000g of gelatin capsules suitable for herbal supplements.
Grind 500mg of dried hydrastis canadensis root into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix the ground hydrastis canadensis root powder with a small amount of water to form a paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with the hydrastis canadensis root paste using a capsule filler machine.
Label and store the hydrastis canadensis capsules in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with cystitis because they provide natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that soothe and calm the urinary tract. The capsular form of elderberry is easily absorbed into the bloodstream, allowing it to quickly reach the affected area and begin to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort. Elder's antiseptic properties also help to combat bacteria that can cause cystitis, promoting a healthy balance in the urinary tract and reducing the risk of infection recurrence.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Elderberry flavonoids, such as anthocyanins and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections in the urinary tract that contribute to cystitis.

Antioxidants: Elderberry antioxidants, including vitamins C and E, help protect the bladder lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and promoting healing.

Ursolic acid: Ursolic acid, a triterpenoid found in elderberry, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce symptoms such as pain, burning, and urgency associated with cystitis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help with cystitis by reducing inflammation in the urinary tract and improving the overall immune response to infections.
Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat bacterial infections and alleviate symptoms associated with cystitis.
Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help soothe and protect the urinary tract, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to cystitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract.
Leaves: They are used due to their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to increase urine production and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract.
Barks: They are used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh sambucus nigra flowers at the peak of bloom to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the flowers in a well-ventilated area at 40°c for 3 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Crush 50g of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to enhance extraction.
Mix the powder with 10g of tapioca starch and 10g of gelatin in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to prevent oxidation and contamination.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover capsules helps with cystitis because they contain isoflavones, a type of plant-based estrogen that has anti-inflammatory properties. These isoflavones have been shown to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with urinary tract infections (UTIs), including the burning sensation while urinating and frequent trips to the bathroom. Additionally, red clover capsules may also help to soothe the bladder lining and reduce symptoms such as pain or pressure in the lower abdomen.
remedy_properties: Isflavones: Isoflavones, particularly formononetin and biochanin A, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of cystitis.

Phytoestrogens: Phytoestrogens, such as coumestrol, mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that may contribute to cystitis and relieve symptoms like frequency and urgency.

Antioxidants: Red clover capsules contain a range of antioxidants, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which can help protect the urinary tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by inflammation, reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and fibrosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the red clover plant contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and treat the urinary tract.
Flowers: The flowers of the red clover plant are rich in isoflavones, which have estrogen-like properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality red clover flowers trifolium pratense, ensuring no contamination or pesticides used in farming practices.
Dry the collected flowers using a low-temperature oven or food dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried red clover flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to ensure uniform size.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with a dosage of 250 milligrams of the powdered trifolium pratense, sealing each capsule tightly to prevent contamination.
Store the finished herbal capsules in an airtight container, kept in a cool dark place for up to six months or frozen for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with cystitis because they contain the natural anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow leaves, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract. The capsules may also help to combat bacterial infections that can contribute to cystitis by increasing urine production and flushing out bacteria from the bladder. Additionally, yarrow's antimicrobial properties can help to prevent the growth of harmful bacteria in the urinary tract, providing long-lasting relief from symptoms such as burning sensations while urinating and frequent trips to the bathroom.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Yarrow capsules contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of cystitis.

Antimicrobial activity: The active compounds in yarrow capsules exhibit antimicrobial effects against bacteria such as E. coli, which can cause cystitis, helping to prevent infections and promote healing.

Strengthening and toning properties: Yarrow capsules contain saponins that help strengthen the lining of the urinary tract, reducing susceptibility to infection and irritation, while also toning the muscles in the pelvic region to improve bladder function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
Buds: Yarrow buds are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried achillea millefolium leaves and stems in sufficient quantity for desired dosage.
Measure one teaspoon of the dried herb and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Mix the ground powder with 100mg of gelatin and 250ml of distilled water to create a gel-like mixture.
Fill empty capsules with 300-400mg of the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container and take two capsules three times daily for one week.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with cystitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the irritated urinary tract tissues. The capsules contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with cystitis. Additionally, licorice root has antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to the development of cystitis. By targeting both inflammation and infection, herbal licorice capsules provide relief from symptoms such as burning sensation while urinating, frequent urination, and pelvic pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal licorice contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the urinary tract, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with cystitis.

Antimicrobial properties: Licorice has antimicrobial effects that can help combat bacterial infections, such as E. coli, that often cause cystitis, reducing the risk of complications and speeding up recovery.

Sothisticating and anti-oxidant properties: Herbal licorice contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and isoflavones, which can soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing irritation and inflammation, while also protecting against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, an active compound that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to soothe and treat cystitis symptoms.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation and pain associated with cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots and dry them in a warm oven at 60°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the powdered root with 250mg of cornstarch and 50mg of silicon dioxide as excipients.
Fill 100-capsule gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately after filling.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with cystitis because they contain the essential oil of Eucalyptus globulus, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with cystitis. The capsules also have antimicrobial effects, which can help combat the bacteria that cause urinary tract infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. By supporting the body's natural defenses, these capsules can provide effective relief from symptoms such as burning urination and frequent urination.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in the ratio of one part leaves to two parts carrier herb like peppermint or marshmallow root.
Dry the mixture in a low-temperature oven set at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve the medicinal properties.
Crush the dried mixture into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder to enhance absorption.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one-quarter teaspoon of the eucalyptus globulus powder mixed with water to form a smooth paste, then seal the capsule.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months and take 2 capsules thrice daily as needed for relief from cystitis symptoms.

Mix 500 milligrams of the ground valeriana officinalis powder with 1000 milliliters of boiling water to create an infusion that steeps for 30 minutes.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cystitis that are treated with herbal capsules are urinary tract infections (UTIs), prostatitis, and urethritis. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like saw palmetto, stinging nettle, and goldenseal may help alleviate symptoms such as burning sensation while urinating, frequency of urination, and discomfort in the pelvic area. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that may help soothe and heal the urinary tract.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with cystitis is a blend of Juniper berries, Uva Ursi, and Corn silk. Juniper berries have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease pain, while Uva Ursi helps to reduce bacterial growth and soothe the urinary tract. Corn silk, rich in antioxidants, calms inflammation and promotes healing. Taking these capsules together can provide relief from cystitis symptoms, such as burning and urgency, and support the body's natural recovery process. Consult a healthcare professional before adding any supplements to your routine.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Urologiia (Moscow, Russia : 1999), herbal capsules, specifically Phytolysin nefroCAPS, are beneficial for cystitis due to their wide direct or indirect antimicrobial effect, anti-inflammatory properties, and antispasmodic effects. The study found that the combination of antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects in Phytolysin nefroCAPS is particularly beneficial for treating uncomplicated urinary tract infections, including cystitis. Additionally, the co-administration of Phytolysin nefroCAPS with an antibacterial drug was found to be more effective than antibacterial therapy alone in patients with chronic recurrent cystitis.
